dialogos.texto_en: Hey, Futaro.\nHear something?
It's music.\nIs someone playing nearby?
I can hear it coming from over there.\nShall we go check it out?
Ah!\nThe fountain water...!
Is it moving to the rythm of the music?\nWow, I didn't know they had events like this.
(I can hear Okinawan folk music.\n The sound of the traditional three-stringed instrument\n is really pleasant.)
This is like, really Okinawan, huh.
Teehee! I know what you mean when you say that.\nThe music and the atmosphere\nare so reminiscent of Okinawa.
I wonder how this type of music came about.\nIt must have a long history.
It was originally music for the gods,\nor for the court...\nI'm not exactly sure.
Oh, so that was music for praying to the gods.\nNow that you mention it, it does have that feeling.
Alright, then....
(She's putting her hands together...\n is she praying?)
I took this opportunity\nto pray to the gods for our happiness, Futaro.
Here? I don't think this is a prayer spot.
I know that.
But, I feel like praying right now.\nIt's not about the place, it's about the feeling.
I pray that I can always be with you, Futaro,\nand that we can be happy together.
Is that so?
Guess I'll pray too.
You too, Futaro?\nThat doesn't seem like something you'd do.
Well, even if I don't believe in any god...
Making a promise in front of you can give me motivation\nto make it happen in reality.
I-I see...\nIt's more than a prayer...it's like a promise.
Then let's make a promise together,\nyou and I, Futaro.
To always be together, and to be happy.
You don't need to do that.\nI mean...
I'll make you happy, I promise.
Futaro...!
Nah, woah, what am I saying!\nSorry, forget I said anything!\nI said something really weird! Hahaha!
No, I won't forget that.
And it wasn't weird.\nIt's something only you can do for me, Futaro.
Because, my happiness can't exist without you.
Nino...
Make me happy, Futaro.
Y-yeah! Of course!
I promise to make you happy too, Futaro.
Hm?\nThe music stopped, is it over now?
Seems so.\nI kinda wanted to listen a little more, though.
There seem to be various other events in this park.
Instead of looking around the facilities,\nI wonder if we can find something just by taking a walk?
Considering its size,\nit might even take a whole day to explore.
But even that one day will probably end in a flash\nif I'm with you, Futaro.
That makes me think of Einstein.
Einstein?\nWhat are you talking about?
N-no, it's nothing.\nAnyway, shall we try going somewhere else?
